# Cartel Capture

> Cartel Capture is an independent investigative documentary channel producing free, sourced documentaries on cartel networks, political corruption, organised crime, and state violence across Latin America and beyond. All content is published on YouTube and fully free to watch.

## About

Cartel Capture investigates how criminal organisations gain and maintain power — following the money, naming the players, and showing how ordinary people pay the price for extraordinary greed. Every claim is supported by verifiable sources, cited publicly on the website. The channel is 100% independent with no advertiser or political influence.

## Videos — Live on YouTube

- [How Special Forces Training Created Los Zetas](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dhRmWg1AFkU): Documents how elite Mexican military units trained by the United States defected to form Los Zetas, one of the most violent cartels in history. Covers cartel formation, drug trafficking routes, and the role of state institutions.

- [The Most Brutal Woman in Narco History](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RFRCb5ALUog): Profiles the most feared woman in Latin American organised crime, examining the intersection of cartel leadership, gender, and political protection.

- [Pinochet's Kill Team Operated Inside the U.S. Capital](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zyhLyU6dGAE): Investigates how Pinochet's secret police (DINA) carried out the 1976 car bomb assassination of Orlando Letelier on the streets of Washington D.C. — an act of state terror on U.S. soil.

## Videos — Coming Soon

- The SS Officer: King of Cocaine — How a wanted Nazi war criminal became a drug lord's enforcer in post-war South America.
- The Bribery Office — Investigates a systematic government bribery operation and the officials who ran it.

## Topics Covered

- Criminal networks and cartel structures
- Political capture and corruption
- Money laundering through real estate, shell companies, and banks
- State terror and government-sponsored violence
- Communities living under territorial cartel control
- Impunity and the failure of accountability systems

## Regions

South America, Central America, Mexico, and cases involving U.S. and European institutions.

## Sources & Citations

All investigations cite primary and secondary sources including: InSight Crime, UNODC, DEA, Reuters, El País, The Intercept, the National Security Archive (GWU), and official government reports. Full citations are published at: https://cartelcapture.tv
